Defines a function to reset a fake settings file for testing purposes.

# Purpose
This C header file declares a single function, [`fake_settings_file_reset`](<#fake_settings_file_reset>). The function is likely intended to reset or initialize a settings file to a default state. ### fake\_settings\_file\_reset<!-- {{#callable_declaration:fake_settings_file_reset}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../tests/fakes/fake_settings_file.h#L19>)

Resets the fake settings file to its initial state.
- **Description**: Use this function to clear all entries in the fake settings file and reset its state. It releases any allocated memory for keys and values, sets all lengths to zero, marks all entries as not dirty, and closes the settings file. Call this function when you need to reinitialize the settings file or ensure it is in a clean state before starting new operations.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: None
- **See Also**: [`fake_settings_file_reset`](<fake_settings_file.c.md#fake_settings_file_reset>)  (Implementation)
